issues.title: 19 Unused Functions
issues.desc: Functions not called from other files

items.name: get_demo_files
items.code:
def get_demo_files():
    """20 sample file operations"""
    
    base_time = datetime.now()
    
    return [
        {"operation": "sorted", "filename": "Q1_Report_2026.pdf", "category": "Reports", "size_mb": 2.4, "timestamp": (base_time - timedelta(hours=1)).isoformat()},
        {"operation": "sorted", "filename": "Invoice_March_2026.xlsx", "category": "Finance/Invoices", "size_mb": 0.3, "timestamp": (base_time - timedelta(hours=2)).isoformat()},
        {"operation": "sorted", "filename": "Employment_Contract_Smith.pdf", "category": "Legal/Contracts", "size_mb": 1.1, "timestamp": (base_time - timedelta(hours=3)).isoformat()},
        {"operation": "sorted", "filename": "Presentation_Deck_v2.pptx", "category": "Presentations", "size_mb": 15.7, "timestamp": (base_time - timedelta(hours=4)).isoformat()},
        {"operation": "sorted", "filename": "receipt_amazon_0320.pdf", "category": "Finance/Receipts", "size_mb": 0.1, "timestamp": (base_time - timedelta(hours=5)).isoformat()},
        {"operation": "sorted", "filename": "team_photo.jpg", "category": "Images", "size_mb": 4.2, "timestamp": (base_time - timedelta(hours=6)).isoformat()},
        {"operation": "sorted", "filename": "Budget_Tracker_2026.xlsx", "category": "Spreadsheets", "size_mb": 0.8, "timestamp": (base_time - timedelta(hours=8)).isoformat()},
        {"operation": "sorted", "filename": "Meeting_Notes_0315.txt", "category": "Correspondence", "size_mb": 0.02, "timestamp": (base_time - timedelta(hours=10)).isoformat()},
        {"operation": "sorted", "filename": "Tax_Form_W9_2026.pdf", "category": "Finance/Tax", "size_mb": 0.5, "timestamp": (base_time - timedelta(days=1)).isoformat()},
  // ...

items.name: log_learning
items.code:
def log_learning(action: str, detail: str, level: str = "DEBUG") -> None:
    """Log learning events"""
    logger = get_logger("learning")
    log_func = getattr(logger, level.lower(), logger.info)
    log_func(f"[LEARNING] {action}: {detail}")

items.name: log_system
items.code:
def log_system(action: str, detail: str, level: str = "INFO") -> None:
    """Log system events"""
    logger = get_logger("system")
    log_func = getattr(logger, level.lower(), logger.info)
    log_func(f"[SYSTEM] {action}: {detail}")

issues.title: 3 Architecture Violations
issues.desc: Lower layers importing from higher layers

items.name: utils → ui
items.suggestion: utils should not import from ui. Consider inverting the dependency or using dependency injection.

items.name: config → data
items.suggestion: config should not import from data. Consider inverting the dependency or using dependency injection.

items.name: test → utils
items.suggestion: test should not import from utils. Consider inverting the dependency or using dependency injection.

patterns.name: Factory
patterns.desc: Creates objects without specifying exact class. Enables loose coupling and extensibility.

patterns.name: Observer/Event
patterns.desc: Defines a subscription mechanism for event-driven architecture. Great for decoupling.

patterns.name: Route Decorators
patterns.desc: Flask/FastAPI/Django route decorators for URL routing. Common in Python web frameworks.

patterns.name: Dataclasses
patterns.desc: Python dataclasses for structured data. Reduces boilerplate for data-holding classes.

patterns.name: Django Signals
patterns.desc: Django signals for decoupled event-driven communication between components.

patterns.name: God Object
patterns.desc: Files with too many responsibilities (15+ functions). Consider splitting into smaller modules.

patterns.name: Long File
patterns.desc: Files over 500 lines are harder to maintain. Consider breaking into smaller modules.

patterns.name: VBA God Module
patterns.desc: VBA modules with 20+ procedures. Consider splitting into smaller modules.

security.title: Hardcoded Secret
security.desc: Credentials should never be hardcoded. Use environment variables or a secrets manager.

security.title: OS Command Execution
security.desc: os.system()/os.popen() are vulnerable to command injection. Use subprocess with shell=False.

security.title: Bare Except Clauses
security.desc: 3 bare except: clauses found. These catch all exceptions including SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt.
